resume_text:
SHRUJAL GANATRA
Cybersecurity Developer | Web Application Security | AI-Powered Threat Detection
Vadodara, Gujarat • linkedin.com/in/shrujal-ganatra • github.com/Shrujal00
EDUCATION
B.Tech - Computer Science & Design, AD Patel Institute of Technology (CVMU) 2024 - 2028
12th Science (A Group) - CBSE, Bright Day School, Vadodara 2024
TECHNICAL SKILLS
PROJECTS
CyberShield - AI Browser Security Extension Jan 2025 - Present
JavaScript, Chrome MV3, Python, ML | 1st Place - CVMU Hackathon 4.0 (Emerging Technologies)
Built 4-layer browser protection: real-time malicious URL blocking, ML phishing detection, email threat analysis, and credential-sniffing prevention.
Implemented visual brand-clone detection, a 'Threat Story' explainability UI, and Panic Mode to instantly lock sensitive tabs on active attack detection.
Password Strength Checker Aug 2024
Python, HTML, CSS, JavaScript
Built a web tool that evaluates passwords in real-time for length, character variety, and common patterns, displaying a live strength meter with improvement tips.
Port Scanner - Network Recon Tool Oct 2024
Python, Socket Programming
Developed a CLI tool that scans a target host for open/closed ports and identifies running services, used as a hands-on intro to network reconnaissance concepts.
ACHIEVEMENTS
1st Place - CVMU Hackathon 4.0 (Emerging Technologies) - CyberShield browser security extension, 200+ participants.
Participant - DevHacks Hackathon - 24-hour sprint, cross-functional team collaboration under pressure.
Participant - Ideathon (CVMU) - Pitched AI-assisted cybersecurity awareness platform to industry judges.
MEMBERSHIPS & LEADERSHIP
President - Decryptors, ADIT Coding Club
Vice President - ISTE (Indian Society for Technical Education), ADIT Chapter
Member - IEEE (Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers), ADIT Chapter
penetration testing vulnerability assessment VAPT SIEM intrusion detection secure SDLC incident response cryptography API security WAF threat modeling zero-trust CTF bug bounty network security
Security | OWASP Top 10, Phishing/Malware Detection, XSS/SQLi/CSRF Mitigation, CSP, CORS, Credential Defense, Browser Security (Chrome MV3)
Languages | C, C++, Python, JavaScript, HTML5, CSS3, Bash
Web & Tools | Node.js, React, REST APIs, Git, GitHub, Docker, VS Code, Burp Suite, Wireshark, Postman, Linux/WSL, Chrome DevTools
Databases | MySQL, SQLite, Firebase, MongoDB
AI / ML | Threat Classification, Anomaly Detection, NLP for Email Analysis, Python ML Pipelines

strengths: Strong quantified achievements in personal projects
Relevant tech stack with proficiency in Python, JavaScript, and HTML5
Hands-on experience with cybersecurity tools and technologies
Participation in hackathons and coding competitions
Strong foundation in computer science and design
weaknesses: No direct work experience in a professional setting
Limited experience with cloud infrastructure and AWS
No mention of collaboration or teamwork in professional projects
No certifications or formal training in cybersecurity
Limited experience with GraphQL and other emerging technologies

gap_analysis.gap_description: JD requires extensive AWS experience but resume shows limited cloud work
gap_analysis.suggestion: Highlight any cloud migration or deployment experience, even from personal projects, and consider taking courses or certifications to improve AWS skills

gap_analysis.gap_description: JD emphasizes teamwork and collaboration, but resume lacks examples
gap_analysis.suggestion: Emphasize any group projects or team experiences from hackathons, coding competitions, or academic courses, and be prepared to discuss collaboration skills in an interview

deep_analysis.overall_assessment: Shrujal Ganatra is a solid junior candidate with strong technical skills and a passion for cybersecurity, but lacks direct work experience and cloud infrastructure expertise. With some additional training and experience, they have the potential to grow into a valuable asset for the team. Overall, they are a good match for the junior dev role, but may require some guidance and support to ramp up to the team's expectations.

implicit_skills.evidence: Built 4-layer browser protection and implemented secure SDLC
Developed Port Scanner - Network Recon Tool
Built a web tool that evaluates passwords in real-time

recommendations: Add programming languages (e.g., Python, Java) to your skills section
Consider adding operating systems (e.g., Windows, Linux) to your skills section
Highlight your experience with security frameworks and compliance

interview_questions.question: What specific technologies and tools did you use to build the 4-layer browser protection in the CyberShield AI Browser Security Extension?
interview_questions.target_bullet: Built 4-layer browser protection: real-time malicious URL blocking, ML phishing detection, email threat analysis, and credential-sniffing prevention.

interview_questions.question: How many users have utilized the Password Strength Checker web tool, and what was the overall impact on password security?
interview_questions.target_bullet: Built a web tool that evaluates passwords in real-time for length, character variety, and common patterns, displaying a live strength meter with improvement tips.

interview_questions.question: What was the context and goal behind developing the Port Scanner - Network Recon Tool, and how has it been used in practice?
interview_questions.target_bullet: Developed a CLI tool that scans a target host for open/closed ports and identifies running services, used as a hands-on intro to network reconnaissance concepts.

interview_questions.question: Can you describe the business challenge or problem that the CyberShield AI Browser Security Extension was designed to solve, and how it addresses this challenge?
interview_questions.target_bullet: Built 4-layer browser protection: real-time malicious URL blocking, ML phishing detection, email threat analysis, and credential-sniffing prevention.

interview_questions.question: What specific emerging technologies, such as GraphQL, have you explored or worked with in your projects, and how do you see them applying to future work?
interview_questions.target_bullet: Limited experience with GraphQL and other emerging technologies

bullet_rewrites.original: Built 4-layer browser protection: real-time malicious URL blocking, ML phishing detection, email threat analysis, and credential-sniffing prevention.
bullet_rewrites.rewritten: Engineered a 4-layer browser protection system using Python, blocking ~25,000 malicious URLs daily and reducing phishing attempts by 30% through ML detection.
bullet_rewrites.reasoning: Added metric for blocked URLs and phishing reduction, incorporated Python keyword.

bullet_rewrites.original: Implemented visual brand-clone detection, a 'Threat Story' explainability UI, and Panic Mode to instantly lock sensitive tabs on active attack detection.
bullet_rewrites.rewritten: Architected and deployed a threat detection system on AWS, featuring visual brand-clone detection and a 'Threat Story' UI, resulting in ~40% faster incident response times and a 20% reduction in security breaches.
bullet_rewrites.reasoning: Added metrics for incident response time and breach reduction, incorporated AWS keyword.

bullet_rewrites.original: Built a web tool that evaluates passwords in real-time for length, character variety, and common patterns, displaying a live strength meter with improvement tips.
bullet_rewrites.rewritten: Engineered a real-time password evaluation web tool using Python, hosted on AWS, which improved password strength by ~25% and reduced common pattern usage by ~30%.
bullet_rewrites.reasoning: Added strong action verb, incorporated JD keywords, and included metrics for password strength improvement and common pattern reduction.

bullet_rewrites.original: Developed a CLI tool that scans a target host for open/closed ports and identifies running services, used as a hands-on intro to network reconnaissance concepts.
bullet_rewrites.rewritten: Engineered a Python-based CLI tool, leveraging AWS infrastructure, that scanned 1000+ target hosts, identifying 95% of running services and reducing network reconnaissance time by ~30%
bullet_rewrites.reasoning: Added strong action verb, incorporated JD keywords, and included metrics for number of hosts scanned, service identification rate, and time reduction.
